name: Vertical AI Workflow Systems
whatTheyAreSaying: Partners are framing the strongest AI companies as workflow systems for specific job functions, not generic chat products.
whyItMatters: This points LAUNCH toward founder discovery in narrow operational markets where AI can become the system of record after solving a painful wedge workflow.

name: AI Infrastructure for Agent Reliability
whatTheyAreSaying: Partners are talking about inference cost, evals, routing, observability, security, and orchestration as the next AI infrastructure layers.
whyItMatters: As enterprises move from pilots to production agents, reliability tooling becomes budget-bearing infrastructure instead of experimental developer tooling.

name: AI Agents for Enterprise Workflows
whatTheyAreSaying: Partners and founders are increasingly discussing AI agents moving from copilots into workflow ownership with human checkpoints.
whyItMatters: Investor interest appears to be shifting toward AI-native software that can automate measurable business functions and show clear ROI in weeks.

name: Agentic DevTools and Repo Ownership
whatTheyAreSaying: VC partners are describing coding agents as long-running owners of repo maintenance, debugging, deployment, and compliance work.
whyItMatters: DevTools remain a LAUNCH-friendly category when the product shortens expensive engineering loops and has obvious bottoms-up adoption.

name: Healthcare AI for Administrative Burden
whatTheyAreSaying: YC and Sequoia signals emphasize AI embedded in healthcare operations: nurse workflows, care coordination, billing, and revenue cycle automation.
whyItMatters: Administrative healthcare workflows have urgent labor pressure, clear budgets, and measurable outcomes, making them strong research targets for seed-stage founders.

name: Robotics and Physical AI Data Loops
whatTheyAreSaying: YC is calling for robotics founders while Sequoia partners emphasize real-world deployment, production workflows, and operational learning loops.
whyItMatters: Physical AI is moving from impressive demos toward constrained commercial deployments, where early customer pilots can reveal investable wedge markets.

name: Defense Software and Autonomous Systems
whatTheyAreSaying: Partners are describing defense as a modern software market where product velocity, procurement fluency, autonomy, and secure communications matter.
whyItMatters: The category is becoming more accessible to software-native founders, but diligence should focus on procurement access and urgent customer pull.
